name: J. J. Johnson
description: American jazz trombonist, composer and arranger (1924–2001)

quotes.text: Yes. In the next few weeks – as a matter of fact, the exact date is April 6, 1994 – there will be a celebration of 50 years of Jazz at the Philharmonic at Carnegie Hall – a black tie, high profile, prestigious event. They are even calling it the musical event of the year at Carnegie Hall in 1994. We’ll see about that. But a lot of people are excited about it. I will be involved. Illinois Jacquet, who was certainly a part of Jazz at the Philharmonic, will be there. A lot of people are on it that were not a part of Jazz at the Philharmonic, to make it a very high profile, prestigious event. I’m told that Shirley Horne is going to be . . . Carolyn, who are some of the other people who are going to be on the Carnegie Hall thing April the 6th? Shirley Horne and who else? CAROLYN JOHNSON: Abbey Lincoln.
quotes.caption: JJ Johnson recalls performing at the 1994 Jazz at the Philharmonic 50th anniversary celebration
quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

quotes.text: Abbey Lincoln. A lot of people. A lot of people. I have the article here. I'll show it to you. It's interesting. It can explain in much more detail about what April the 6th at Carnegie Hall is all about than I can ever do. We saved it just for that purpose, 'cause I’ve tried a couple of times to explain to people and they say, "I don’t know what you're talking about." [Then I say,] "Here, read this." [And then they say,] "Oh! Now we know what you mean."
quotes.caption: JJ Johnson recalls the April 6, 1994 Verve Records concert
quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

quotes.text: This is true. Norman Granz came into our lives with his whole Jazz at the Philharmonic enterprise that was quite innovative at that time. No one had ever done what Norman Granz wanted to do. And for the record, getting ahead of our current cycle of time, you may or may not know that in the next few weeks there's going to be a tribute to 50 years of Jazz at the Philharmonic at Carnegie Hall. [telephone keeps ringing]
quotes.caption: Mentions a future tribute to Jazz at the Philharmonic
quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Actually, J.J. Johnson joined the band in 1942, in September, when we were on the way to California, we picked up J.J. Johnson in Indianapolis.

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: So I went and worked with Sonny Rollins at Birdland for a week and then after that I joined J.J. Johnson’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: So after Tony Scott I started working with Kai Winding and J.J. Johnson... I made about six albums with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: And while I was there I played with Miles, I played with JJ, I played with Kye Windin...
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: We did a tour … I first met him when he had his band in Rochester … Subsequent to that, we did a tour with Friedrich Gulda … this particular band, including the Europeans were Mel Lewis, me, J.J., Freddie Hubbard and Sahib Shahab.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Turré
about_quotes.evidence: in high school somebody gave me a J.J. Johnson record and it was all over, man. I didn’t even know you could play a trombone like that... But J.J., I said, oh man, that’s where it’s at. So I started to get into that music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Turré interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 29, 2021
